name: Kumquat-Pecan Crepes
recipeInstructions:
1. Seed, chop and pat dry the kumquats, reserving 1/3 cup of kumquat syrup.
2. Combine eggs, pecans, sugar, butter, kumquats, and cognac in a processor or blender. Mix well, turning on and off.
3. Transfer to a bowl and cover. Freeze for at least 1 hour (filling can be prepared ahead and frozen).
4. Generously butter two 7x11-inch baking dishes. Reserve 1/3 cup of filling for sauce.
5. Fill each crepe with about 1 1/2 to 2 tablespoons of filling. Roll the crepes up cigar fashion.
6. Arrange seam side down in a single layer in the prepared baking dishes. This can be done 1 day ahead, covered and refrigerated, or frozen for several days.
7. Preheat oven to 350°F.
8. Sprinkle crepes with remaining pecans, sugar, and melted butter. Bake until bubbling hot, about 15 minutes.
9. In a small saucepan, combine the reserved filling, 2 tablespoons cognac, and 1/3 cup kumquat syrup. Bring to simmer over low heat.
10. Warm the remaining cognac in a small saucepan.
11. Arrange crepes on a platter and top with sauce. Turn off the oven. Pour the warmed cognac over the top and serve immediately.
